PLAYLIST.WEBPLAYER_URL_COPY_TO_CLIPBOARD: The copied WebPlayer url is valid for 30 seconds.

INFO.RESTART_TO_APPLY_CHANGES: You need to restart to apply changes.
INFO.PROCESS_PARALLEL: Process Parallel: If you have multiple providers, you can enable it. Accessing your provider in parallel can cause a ban.
INFO.WEB_ROOT: Web-Root: Contains the web-ui files. They are normally placed in the same directory under ./web

USER.TARGET_FORCE_REDIRECT: Force Redirect is set for target

PROXY.RESOURCE_REWRITE_DISABLE: If activated, the URL rewrite of resources in reverse proxy mode is disabled
PROXY.DISABLED_HEADER: Remove headers before sending requests to the upstream provider
PROXY.REFERER_HEADER: If activated, the referer header is not sent to the provider
PROXY.X_HEADER: If activated, headers starting with 'X-' are removed before proxying the request
PROXY.CUSTOM_HEADERS: Headers listed here are removed before sending the upstream request

WEB_UI.PATH: Configuration of a path prefix, allowing it to be accessed behind a reverse proxy under a custom subpath. like https://yourdomain.com/tuliprox

PANEL.INFO: Configure provisioning via a generic provider panel API per input. Use query param value 'auto' for api_key and credentials where required.
PANEL.ENABLE: Enable panel API for this input to configure provisioning.

DESC.WELCOME: Create the first WebUI user. These credentials will be written to user.txt.
DESC.API: Configure API and API proxy settings.
DESC.API_USERS: Configure API users and target credentials.

MSG.WELCOME: Welcome to tuliprox setup. This wizard will guide you through the initial setup process.
MSG.WEBUI_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH: WebUI password must be at least 8 characters
MSG.COMPLETE_RESTART_HINT: Setup completed successfully. Restart the application/container to continue.

API_PROXY_CONFIG.USE_USER_DB: If enabled, API proxy users are loaded from the user database.

API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.HOST: Upstream host that this proxy server profile points to.
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.MESSAGE: Response message returned by this proxy server profile.
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.NAME: Display name used to identify this API proxy server profile.
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.PATH: Optional path prefix used when routing to this proxy server profile.
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.PORT: Optional upstream port used by this proxy server profile.
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.PROTOCOL: Protocol used by this API proxy server endpoint (for example http or https).
API_PROXY_SERVER_INFO.TIMEZONE: Timezone applied by this proxy server profile for time-aware responses.

Backoff jitter percent must be between 0: No dedicated explanation is available for this field yet.

CACHE_CONFIG.DIR: Filesystem directory where playlist and metadata caches are stored.
CACHE_CONFIG.ENABLED: Globally enables or disables disk caching for input resources.
CACHE_CONFIG.SIZE: Maximum size (in bytes) allowed for the cache directory.

CONFIG_API.HOST: Host where the tuliprox API listens. Default: 0.0.0.0
CONFIG_API.PORT: TCP port where the tuliprox API listens. Default: 8901
CONFIG_API.WEB_ROOT: Filesystem path that serves the frontend assets for the API endpoint. Default: ./web

CONFIG_INPUT.CACHE_DURATION: Playlist cache duration (e.g. 12h, 1d, 30m). Cached playlists are reused to reduce downloads.
CONFIG_INPUT.ENABLED: If disabled, the processing for this input is skipped.
CONFIG_INPUT.EXP_DATE: Manual expiration date for this input (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format or Unix timestamp).
CONFIG_INPUT.MAX_CONNECTIONS: Limit of concurrent connections for this input. 0 means unlimited.
CONFIG_INPUT.NAME: Human readable name for this entry. Must be unique.
CONFIG_INPUT.PASSWORD: Password or credential used for this account or service.
CONFIG_INPUT.PRIORITY: The order of processing. Lower numbers mean higher priority (e.g. -1 is higher than 0).
CONFIG_INPUT.URL: Target URL for this input. Supports provider:// scheme for failover.
CONFIG_INPUT.USERNAME: Login username for this account or service.

CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.ENABLED: Enables or disables this specific alias.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.EXP_DATE: Expiration date for this alias connection.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.MAX_CONNECTIONS: Maximum number of simultaneous connections allowed for this specific alias.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.NAME: Human readable name for this entry.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.PASSWORD: Password or credential used for this account or service.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.PRIORITY: Priority of this alias when failing over. Lower number means higher priority.
CONFIG_INPUT_ALIAS.USERNAME: Login username for this account or service.

CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.PROBE_DELAY: Delay in seconds between probe requests (default 2) to reduce provider pressure.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.PROBE_LIVE: Enables background technical analysis for live channels to detect codecs and resolution.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.PROBE_LIVE_INTERVAL_HOURS: Interval in hours between re-probing live channels (default: 120).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.PROBE_SERIES: Enables technical analysis for series entries (requires ffprobe and probe_stream).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.PROBE_VOD: Enables technical analysis for VOD entries (requires ffprobe and probe_stream).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.RESOLVE_BACKGROUND: If true, metadata resolve tasks are processed in the background (default true).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.RESOLVE_DELAY: Delay in seconds between metadata resolve requests (default 2).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.RESOLVE_SERIES: Enables metadata resolution (TMDB/NFO) for series entries.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.RESOLVE_TMDB: Attempts to resolve missing TMDB IDs based on title if missing from provider.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.RESOLVE_VOD: Enables metadata resolution (TMDB/NFO) for VOD entries.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.XTREAM_LIVE_STREAM_USE_PREFIX: If true, adds /live/ prefix to Xtream stream links (default true).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.XTREAM_LIVE_STREAM_WITHOUT_EXTENSION: If true, removes .ts extension from Xtream stream links (default false).
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.XTREAM_SKIP_LIVE: If true, the live section of the Xtream provider is skipped during update.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.XTREAM_SKIP_SERIES: If true, the series section of the Xtream provider is skipped during update.
CONFIG_INPUT_OPTIONS.XTREAM_SKIP_VOD: If true, the VOD section of the Xtream provider is skipped during update.

CONFIG_TARGET.ENABLED: If disabled, the processing for this target output is skipped.
CONFIG_TARGET.MAPPING: List of mapping IDs to apply to this target.
CONFIG_TARGET.NAME: Human readable name for this entry. Used for selective processing via -t CLI arg.
CONFIG_TARGET.USE_MEMORY_CACHE: If enabled, the playlist is cached in RAM to reduce disk overhead (increases memory usage).
CONFIG_TARGET.WATCH: List of group patterns to monitor for changes. Notifications are sent via configured messaging.

CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY_CONFIG.CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTES: Additional CSP attributes appended to the standard policy.
CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY_CONFIG.ENABLED: Enables or disables Content Security Policy headers for the WebUI.

EXPLANATION.DEFAULT: No dedicated explanation is available for this field yet.

DISCORD.TEMPLATE_MESSAGE: Template message body for Discord notifications.

EPG_CONFIG.NAME_PREFIX_SEPARATOR: List of characters (e.g. ':', '|') that separate channel prefixes from the main title during Smart Match.
EPG_CONFIG.STRIP: Terms to ignore during EPG Smart Matching (e.g. '4K', 'HD', 'HEVC').

EPG_SOURCE.LOGO_OVERRIDE: Overrides the channel logos provided by the EPG with the ones from the playlist or external mapping.
EPG_SOURCE.PRIORITY: Determines which EPG source is preferred if multiple sources provide data for the same channel.
EPG_SOURCE.URL: The URL of the XMLTV EPG file.

EXPLANATION.FFPROBE:
FFprobe controls how stream analysis is performed.

Turns technical stream analysis on or off and configures timeouts and probe depths. 
Example:
```
ffprobe:
  enabled: true
  timeout: 60
  analyze_duration: 10s
  probe_size: 10MB
```

FFPROBE_CONFIG.ANALYZE_DURATION:
How much playback time FFprobe is allowed to inspect for VOD/Series.

Higher values can find more details but take longer.

Examples: `10s`, `30s`, `1m`.
FFPROBE_CONFIG.ENABLED:
Turns FFprobe stream analysis on or off.

When off, the app skips technical probing (video/audio details).
FFPROBE_CONFIG.LIVE_ANALYZE_DURATION:
How much playback time FFprobe may inspect for live streams.

Live checks are usually faster with small values.

Examples: `2s`, `5s`, `10s`.
FFPROBE_CONFIG.LIVE_PROBE_SIZE:
How much data FFprobe may read for live streams.

Smaller values reduce load and make checks faster.

Examples: `512KB`, `1MB`, `5MB`.
FFPROBE_CONFIG.PROBE_SIZE:
How much data FFprobe may read for VOD/Series.

Higher values can improve detection but use more bandwidth.

Examples: `1MB`, `5MB`, `10MB`.
FFPROBE_CONFIG.TIMEOUT:
Maximum seconds one FFprobe run may take.

If the stream is too slow, probing stops when this limit is reached.

Leave empty to use the default (60 seconds).

GEO_IP_CONFIG.ENABLED: If enabled, Geo-IP lookup is performed for incoming requests.
GEO_IP_CONFIG.URL: URL to download the Geo-IP database (e.g. MaxMind GeoLite2-City.mmdb).

HD_HOME_RUN_CONFIG.AUTH: Authentication string or token required for HDHomeRun API access.
HD_HOME_RUN_CONFIG.ENABLED: Enables the HDHomeRun target output emulation.
HD_HOME_RUN_CONFIG.SSDP_DISCOVERY: Enables standard SSDP discovery for HDHomeRun emulation on the local network.

HD_HOME_RUN_TARGET_OUTPUT.DEVICE: The virtual Device ID exposed by the HDHomeRun emulation.
HD_HOME_RUN_TARGET_OUTPUT.USERNAME: Login username for this account or service.

INPUT_FORM.ALIASES: Optional aliases used to manage multiple lines from the same provider.
INPUT_FORM.EPG_SOURCES: Associated EPG (Electronic Program Guide) sources for this input.
INPUT_FORM.FETCH_METHOD: HTTP method used to retrieve the input (e.g., GET, POST).
INPUT_FORM.HEADERS: Custom HTTP headers sent when retrieving the input playlist.
INPUT_FORM.INPUT_TYPE: Defines the streaming protocol or source format (e.g. xtream, m3u).

IP_CHECK_CONFIG.PATTERN_IPV4: Optional regex to extract IPv4 address from the response (default matches standard decimal format).
IP_CHECK_CONFIG.PATTERN_IPV6: Optional regex to extract IPv6 address from the response (default matches standard hex format).
IP_CHECK_CONFIG.URL: URL for a service that returns the current public IP (IPv4 or IPv6).
IP_CHECK_CONFIG.URL_IPV4: Dedicated URL to fetch the current public IPv4 address (e.g. https://ipinfo.io/ip).
IP_CHECK_CONFIG.URL_IPV6: Dedicated URL to fetch the current public IPv6 address.

LIBRARY_CONFIG.ENABLED: Globally enables or disables the local media library feature.
LIBRARY_CONFIG.FORMATS: List of metadata formats to write (e.g. 'nfo' for Kodi-compatible sidecars).
LIBRARY_CONFIG.SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS: List of video file extensions (e.g. mp4, mkv) supported by the scanner.

LIBRARY_METADATA_CONFIG.FALLBACK_TO_FILENAME: If NFO or TMDB resolution fails, Tuliprox attempts to guess metadata from the filename.
LIBRARY_METADATA_CONFIG.PATH: Directory where processed library metadata is stored.

LIBRARY_METADATA_READ_CONFIG.JELLYFIN: Reads NFO metadata files formatted for Jellyfin.
LIBRARY_METADATA_READ_CONFIG.PLEX: Reads XML metadata files or sidecars formatted for Plex.

LIBRARY_PLAYLIST_CONFIG.MOVIE_CATEGORY: Category name for local movies in the generated playlist (default 'Local Movies').
LIBRARY_PLAYLIST_CONFIG.SERIES_CATEGORY: Category name for local series in the generated playlist (default 'Local Series').

LOG_CONFIG.LOG_ACTIVE_USER: If enabled, the number of active reverse proxy clients is logged regularly.
LOG_CONFIG.LOG_LEVEL: Sets the verbosity of application logs (e.g. info, debug, error).
LOG_CONFIG.SANITIZE_SENSITIVE_INFO: If enabled, masks passwords and tokens in logs.

M3U_TARGET_OUTPUT.FILENAME: The name of the generated M3U file (e.g. playlist.m3u).
M3U_TARGET_OUTPUT.INCLUDE_TYPE_IN_URL: If true, the stream type (live/vod/series) is explicitly included in the generated URLs.
M3U_TARGET_OUTPUT.MASK_REDIRECT_URL: If enabled, the real provider URL is masked behind a Tuliprox redirect link even in redirect mode.

MAIN_CONFIG.ACCEPT_INSECURE_SSL_CERTIFICATES: If enabled, SSL certificate validation is skipped for all outgoing requests.
MAIN_CONFIG.BACKUP_DIR: Filesystem directory used for configuration backups.
MAIN_CONFIG.CONFIG_HOT_RELOAD: If true, config files are reloaded automatically on change.
MAIN_CONFIG.CUSTOM_STREAM_RESPONSE_PATH: Path to a directory containing custom error response files for stream failures.
MAIN_CONFIG.DEFAULT_USER_AGENT: The User-Agent string used for outgoing requests if not specified elsewhere.
MAIN_CONFIG.DISK_BASED_PROCESSING:
If enabled, input playlists are processed from disk to save RAM.
If you have enough RAM, you can let this option disabled to improve performance.
MAIN_CONFIG.MAPPING_PATH: Path or directory containing mapping configuration files.
MAIN_CONFIG.PROCESS_PARALLEL: If enabled, input playlists are processed in parallel to reduce latency. However this is currently not supported.
MAIN_CONFIG.SLEEP_TIMER_MINS: Automatically closes streams and stops streaming after the specified number of minutes of inactivity.
MAIN_CONFIG.TEMPLATE_PATH: Path or directory containing template configuration files.
MAIN_CONFIG.UPDATE_ON_BOOT: If enabled, Tuliprox triggers a full playlist update immediately upon startup. Helpful during initial setup, but usually set to false for stable operations.
MAIN_CONFIG.USER_ACCESS_CONTROL:
If enabled, the attributes (if set)

- expiration date,
- status and
- max_connections

are checked to permit or deny access for api users.
MAIN_CONFIG.USER_CONFIG_DIR: Directory path where user-specific overrides and configuration files are stored.
MAIN_CONFIG.WORKING_DIR: The primary directory where Tuliprox stores its data files and databases.

MESSAGING_CONFIG.NOTIFY_ON: Select events (e.g. 'info', 'stats', 'error') that trigger notifications for this specific type.
MESSAGING_CONFIG.REST_HEADERS: Custom HTTP headers to send with the request (JSON format).
MESSAGING_CONFIG.TELEGRAM_CHAT_IDS: List of telegram chat IDs to receive notifications. Format: '<chat_id>' or '<chat_id>:<thread_id>'.

METADATA_LOG_CONFIG.LOG_ACTIVE_USER: Logs which WebUI user initiated or is viewing the metadata processing.
METADATA_LOG_CONFIG.PROGRESS_INTERVAL:
How often the app writes progress updates while metadata tasks are running.

Short intervals show more live progress, long intervals keep logs cleaner.

Examples: `10s`, `30s`, `1m`.
METADATA_LOG_CONFIG.QUEUE_INTERVAL:
How often the app writes queue status messages to the log.

Use a smaller value if you want to see activity more often. Use a larger value if logs feel too noisy.

Examples: `15s`, `30s`, `1m`.
METADATA_LOG_CONFIG.SANITIZE_SENSITIVE_INFO: Attempts to mask passwords and tokens in the logging output for security.

METADATA_UPDATE_CONFIG.MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:
Maximum number of metadata tasks kept in memory for one input worker.

When this limit is reached, additional tasks are rejected to protect system stability and memory usage.
METADATA_UPDATE_CONFIG.RETRY_DELAY:
Minimum delay for transient requeue scheduling.

Use this to prevent immediate hot-loop retries when tasks are temporarily deferred.

Use duration units like `2s`, `5s`, `10s`.
METADATA_UPDATE_CONFIG.WORKER_IDLE_TIMEOUT:
How long a metadata worker waits for new tasks while idle before it can stop itself.

A lower value releases resources sooner; a higher value keeps workers warm for bursty workloads.

Use duration units like `30s`, `1m`, `5m`.

OUTPUT_HDHOMERUN_FORM.USE_OUTPUT: Enables or disables output creation specifically for HDHomeRun format.

OUTPUT_M3U_FORM.FILTER: Filter expression used to specify which items should be included in the M3U output.

OUTPUT_STRM_FORM.EXPORT_STYLE: The naming convention used for generating .strm files (e.g. kodi, plex, jellyfin).
OUTPUT_STRM_FORM.FILTER: Filter expression used to specify which items should be exported as .strm files.

OUTPUT_XTREAM_FORM.FILTER: Filter expression used to specify which items should be included in the Xtream output.
OUTPUT_XTREAM_FORM.TRAKT_LISTS: Associates Trakt.tv lists to synchronize with this Xtream target output.

PLAYLIST_EPG_SOURCE_SELECTOR.URL: Source URL used to download EPG data.

PLAYLIST_SOURCE_SELECTOR.PASSWORD: Source password used for loading playlists from a protected provider.
PLAYLIST_SOURCE_SELECTOR.URL: Source URL used to download playlist data.
PLAYLIST_SOURCE_SELECTOR.USERNAME: Source username used for loading playlists from a protected provider.

EXPLANATION.PROBE:
Probe controls technical stream analysis retries.

Use this group to define retry steps, retry limits, and cooldown behavior for FFprobe-based stream checks.

PROBE_CONFIG.COOLDOWN:
Pause time after probe retries are exhausted.

During cooldown, that item is skipped for probing. After cooldown ends, probing can start again.

Examples: `1d`, `3d`, `7d`.
PROBE_CONFIG.MAX_ATTEMPTS:
How many probe tries are allowed before cooldown starts.

Lower values reduce provider load, higher values can recover more transient failures.
PROBE_CONFIG.RETRY_BACKOFF_STEP_1:
Wait time after the first probe failure.

Use this as a gentle first retry step.

Example: `10m`.
PROBE_CONFIG.RETRY_BACKOFF_STEP_2:
Wait time after the second probe failure.

Usually set higher than step 1.

Example: `30m`.
PROBE_CONFIG.RETRY_BACKOFF_STEP_3:
Wait time after the third and later probe failures.

This is the longest regular probe delay before cooldown takes over.

Example: `1h`.
PROBE_CONFIG.RETRY_LOAD_RETRY_DELAY:
Wait time before retrying to load saved retry-state data after a read failure.

This helps when storage is temporarily busy or unavailable.

Examples: `30s`, `1m`, `5m`.

PROXY_CONFIG.PASSWORD: Password or credential used for this account or service.
PROXY_CONFIG.USERNAME: Login username for this account or service.

PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.COMMENT: Optional private note for this user account.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.EPG_REQUEST_TIMESHIFT: Override for EPG timeshift from client request parameters.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.EPG_TIMESHIFT: TimeZone or offset (e.g. +2:00, Europe/Berlin) to adjust EPG times for this user.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.EXP_DATE: Unix timestamp or 'YYYY-MM-DD' when this user access expires.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.MAX_CONNECTIONS: Limit of simultaneous streams for this user. 0 = unlimited.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.PASSWORD: Access password for this user's playlist and streams.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.PLAYLIST: Playlist specifically assigned to the user proxy context.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.PROXY: Proxy access definitions or roles for the designated user.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.SERVER: Target proxy server address or definition.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.STATUS: The active or disabled state of the user proxy credential.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.TOKEN: Unique API token that can be used instead of username/password.
PROXY_USER_CREDENTIALS.UI_ENABLED: If true, this user can log into the simplified WebUI bouquet editor (default true).

RATE_LIMIT_CONFIG.BURST_SIZE: Defines the initial number of available connections before throttling applies (e.g. 10).
RATE_LIMIT_CONFIG.ENABLED: Enables rate limiting per IP for reverse proxy connections.
RATE_LIMIT_CONFIG.PERIOD_MILLIS: Interval in milliseconds at which one connection is replenished (e.g. 500).

EXPLANATION.RESOLVE:
Resolve controls metadata lookup retries.

Use this group to tune how often failed metadata lookups are retried and how long the system should wait between retries. Duration fields support suffixes like '30s', '10m', '1h', '7d'.

RESOLVE_CONFIG.EXHAUSTION_RESET_GAP:
How long the app waits before allowing a fresh resolve cycle for previously exhausted items.

Use higher values to avoid repeatedly hammering the same failing entries.

Examples: `30m`, `1h`, `2h`.
RESOLVE_CONFIG.MAX_ATTEMPTS:
How many resolve tries are allowed for one item in one update cycle.

After this limit, the item is paused for now and retried in a later cycle.
RESOLVE_CONFIG.MAX_RETRY_BACKOFF:
Maximum waiting time between repeated resolve retries.

If something keeps failing, the app waits longer each time, but never longer than this value.

Examples: `1m`, `10m`, `1h`.
RESOLVE_CONFIG.MIN_RETRY_BASE:
Minimum waiting time before a resolve retry.

This prevents very fast retry loops when a source has temporary issues.

Examples: `5s`, `30s`, `1m`.

RESOURCE_RETRY_CONFIG.BACKOFF_MILLIS: The wait time between failed request attempts (e.g. 250ms).
RESOURCE_RETRY_CONFIG.BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER: Multiplies the base backoff after each failed attempt to create an exponential delay.

REST_CONFIG.TEMPLATES: Handlebars templates for REST message bodies.

REVERSE_PROXY_CONFIG.FAILOVER_REDIRECT_PATTERNS: Regex patterns determining which HTTP error responses trigger an automatic failover rotation.
REVERSE_PROXY_CONFIG.RESOURCE_REWRITE_DISABLED: If enabled, URL rewriting for logos and EPG is disabled (also disables caching).
REVERSE_PROXY_CONFIG.REWRITE_SECRET:
Persistent secret for stable resource URLs across restarts. 
Example:
```
reverse_proxy:
  rewrite_secret: A1B2C3D4E5F60718293A4B5C6D7E8F90
```

REVERSE_PROXY_DISABLED_HEADER_CONFIG.CLOUDFLARE_HEADER: Removes Cloudflare-specific headers (like CF-Connecting-IP) before forwarding to the upstream.
REVERSE_PROXY_DISABLED_HEADER_CONFIG.CUSTOM_HEADER: List of custom HTTP headers to unconditionally remove before forwarding.
REVERSE_PROXY_DISABLED_HEADER_CONFIG.REFERER_HEADER: Specifically removes the 'Referer' header from incoming requests.
REVERSE_PROXY_DISABLED_HEADER_CONFIG.X_HEADER: Strips any header beginning with 'X-' (e.g., X-Forwarded-For) from incoming requests.

SCHEDULES_CONFIG.SCHEDULE: Standard cron expression defining when the task runs (e.g. '0 0 8 * * * *').
SCHEDULES_CONFIG.TARGETS: List of target playlists to update during a scheduled PlaylistUpdate task.
SCHEDULES_CONFIG.TYPE: The type of task to perform at the scheduled time (e.g. PlaylistUpdate, LibraryScan).

STAGED_INPUT.HEADERS: Custom HTTP headers sent when retrieving this staged configuration.
STAGED_INPUT.PASSWORD: Password or credential used for this account or service.
STAGED_INPUT.USERNAME: Login username for this account or service.

STREAM_BUFFER_CONFIG.ENABLED: Buffers the stream in memory. Useful if the provider is faster than the consumer player.
STREAM_BUFFER_CONFIG.SIZE: Size of the stream buffer in 8192-byte chunks. A value of 1024 means approximately 8MB.

STREAM_CONFIG.GRACE_PERIOD_HOLD_STREAM: If enabled, keeps the provider stream open briefly after the last client disconnects to allow quick reconnects.
STREAM_CONFIG.GRACE_PERIOD_MILLIS: Duration in milliseconds to allow exceeding max_connections during rapid channel switches (default 2000).
STREAM_CONFIG.GRACE_PERIOD_TIMEOUT_SECS: How many seconds to wait before terminating excess connections after grace period ends (default 5).
STREAM_CONFIG.RETRY: Number of times to retry connecting to the provider on initial stream failure.
STREAM_CONFIG.SHARED_BURST_BUFFER_MB: Initial buffer size in MB to provide immediate data to new clients joining a shared stream.
STREAM_CONFIG.THROTTLE: Enables bandwidth limiting for outgoing streams.
STREAM_CONFIG.THROTTLE_KBPS: Maximum bandwidth in Kilobits per second per stream.

STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.ADD_QUALITY_TO_FILENAME: If true, media quality (e.g. 1080p, 4K) is appended to the generated .strm filename.
STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.CLEANUP: If enabled, the destination directory is emptied before generating new .strm files. Use with caution!
STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.DIRECTORY: The filesystem path where .strm files and folder structures are created.
STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.STRM_PROPS: List of Kodi stream properties (e.g. inputstream=...) to include in .strm files.
STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.UNDERSCORE_WHITESPACE: If enabled, replaces all spaces with underscores in filenames and paths.
STRM_TARGET_OUTPUT.USERNAME: The user whose credentials will be embedded in the .strm URLs.

TARGET_FORM.FILTER: Global filter expression applied to the target before creating explicit outputs.
TARGET_FORM.FORCE_REDIRECT: Forces streams directly to the proxy or the provider instead of resolving individually.
TARGET_FORM.PROCESSING_ORDER: Determines the priority or the sequence in which target channels are matched and listed.

TELEGRAM_CONFIG.TEMPLATES: Handlebars templates for Telegram message bodies.

TMDB_CONFIG.API_KEY:
Your TMDB API key.

You only need this if you want to use your own TMDB account limits.
TMDB_CONFIG.CACHE_DURATION_DAYS:
How many days TMDB results stay cached before refresh.

Longer cache means fewer API calls.
TMDB_CONFIG.COOLDOWN:
Pause after a successful TMDB lookup that returned no match.

This prevents endless re-tries for titles that currently do not exist in TMDB.

Examples: `1d`, `3d`, `7d`.
TMDB_CONFIG.ENABLED:
Enables metadata lookup from TMDB.

Turn this off if you do not want TMDB-based title matching.
TMDB_CONFIG.LANGUAGE:
Preferred language for TMDB titles and metadata.

Example: `en-US`.
TMDB_CONFIG.RATE_LIMIT_MS:
Minimum wait time between TMDB requests in milliseconds.

Higher values are gentler to the TMDB API.

Examples: `250`, `500`, `1000`.

TRAKT_LIST_CONFIG.CATEGORY_NAME: The category name used in the playlist for items from this Trakt list.
TRAKT_LIST_CONFIG.FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD: Similarity threshold for matching Trakt items to provider streams (0-100).
TRAKT_LIST_CONFIG.LIST_SLUG: The slug (part of URL) of the Trakt list to fetch.
TRAKT_LIST_CONFIG.USER: The Trakt username who owns the list.

TRAKT_LIST_FORM.TRAKT_CONTENT_TYPE: Specifies whether to fetch 'movies' or 'shows' from the assigned Trakt list.

VIDEO_CONFIG.EXTENSIONS: List of valid video file extensions (e.g. mp4, avi, mkv). Streams ending in these are classified as video VODs.
VIDEO_CONFIG.HEADERS: Custom HTTP headers applied to video download requests (e.g. User-Agent).
VIDEO_CONFIG.WEB_SEARCH: Template URL for external web search (e.g. IMDb). {} is replaced by the title.

VIDEO_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG.DIRECTORY: The destination directory for downloaded video files.
VIDEO_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG.EPISODE_PATTERN:
Regex with 'episode' capture group to clean up series filenames.
Example:
```
episode_pattern: '.*(?P<episode>[Ss]\\d{1,2}.*?[Ee]\\d{1,2}).*'
```
VIDEO_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG.ORGANIZE_INTO_DIRECTORIES: If enabled, creates subdirectories per movie or series during download.

WEBUI_CONFIG.CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE: Custom CSP (Content Security Policy) directives applied to the Web UI.

WEB_AUTH_CONFIG.ENABLED: Enables or disables authentication for the Web-UI.
WEB_AUTH_CONFIG.ISSUER: The issuer name embedded in JWT auth tokens.
WEB_AUTH_CONFIG.SECRET: The secret key used to sign and verify JWT auth tokens. Changing this logs out everyone.
WEB_AUTH_CONFIG.TOKEN_TTL_MINS: How many minutes a login session remains valid (default 3600).
WEB_AUTH_CONFIG.USERFILE: Path to the text file containing WebUI users (default user.txt).

WEB_UI_CONFIG.ENABLED: Enables or disables the central web-based management interface.
WEB_UI_CONFIG.KICK_SECS: Duration in seconds a kicked user is blocked from reconnecting (default 90).
WEB_UI_CONFIG.PATH: Optional path prefix (e.g. /ui) for reverse proxy integration.
WEB_UI_CONFIG.PLAYER_SERVER: Optional server override used specifically for the built-in web player.
WEB_UI_CONFIG.USER_UI_ENABLED: Enables or disables the simplified bouquet editor for standard users.

XTREAM_TARGET_OUTPUT.SKIP_LIVE_DIRECT_SOURCE: If enabled, live streams are not linked directly but proxied/resolved.
XTREAM_TARGET_OUTPUT.SKIP_SERIES_DIRECT_SOURCE: If enabled, series episodes are not linked directly but proxied/resolved.
XTREAM_TARGET_OUTPUT.SKIP_VIDEO_DIRECT_SOURCE: If enabled, VOD files are not linked directly but proxied/resolved.

COUNTRY.SH: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
COUNTRY.GS: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
